A JavaScript test function that returns 1 if true, otherwise triggers an assertion failure.

# Purpose
This code is a simple JavaScript function named [`test`](<#05-003test>) that demonstrates basic conditional logic. The function checks a condition that is always true and returns the integer `1` when the condition is met. If the condition were false, the code would call an `assert` function with a `false` argument, which is not defined in this code snippet. The function is immediately invoked after its definition, indicating it is intended to execute as part of a script or test. The code provides narrow functionality, primarily serving as a basic example of conditional statements and function invocation.
